What's going on, guys? This video is going to serve as an overarching video behind this guy. What is the 42-day detox? How is it both an internal play and external play? How does the no-sale sell work into it? I just want to cover it from an overarching standpoint. Internal plays are plays that you market to your existing customer base. The 42-day detox serves as an existing play that you can give to your customer base. The reason that this play is going to be even more successful for you is because we've put so much stuff in there, so much amazing value from the cookbooks, the redone meal plans, the fitness videos, all of these things that you would never normally have with an internal play. You're going to be able to give that to your customers. Typically, when we had this type of value, we would sell this as a $1,000 program online over the phone all day long. You're going to give it to your people for nothing. What's going to happen is you're going to have this positive cycle that happens every time we release a play that's this big. They're going to get it for free, and they're going to continue to want to sign up for your internal plays. You create this habit for every quarter you make a big internal play. Everyone signs up, and then you sell them supplements. That's how this works from an internal play standpoint. The 42-day detox works as an early play. You put it to your customers, they sign up for free, and then on the nutrition orientation, you'll sell them supplements. That's how it works. The 42-day detox also, and this is a benefit, is that it also tested very, very well, better than the original six-week challenge, as an external play. Which means this is something that happens rarely, but in the occasion that you have a truly ridiculously high-converting play, you can actually use it to market to cold traffic. You can market the 42-day detox the same way you would for the six-week challenge. You now have two external plays that are high-converting, two cold traffic to give your audience some variation, which is fantastic. That covers, you can market to your internal customers as an internal play, and you can market to your cold traffic to your area as an external play. How does this work together with the no-sale sale? The no-sale sale is purposely only something that goes to external clients. Reason for that is because if you didn't close, you've already sold your internal customers on being at your gym. You did close that sale, so there is no no-sale to be had there. You already made the sale. With people who are coming in from cold traffic, those people, if they come in and then don't sign up for the 42-day detox or even a six-week challenge, it doesn't matter what they're coming in for. You can use the no-sale sale no matter what. The 42-day detox is also a play that you can use the no-sale sale for, but the no-sale sale is not limited to the 42-day detox. Anytime you have someone coming in from cold traffic who walks in your gym who is not a member, if they say no, you can then offer them the 42-day detox or whatever play you want. In this instance, I would recommend the 42-day detox because it works for six weeks, six weeks long, so you could use it to fulfill the six-week challenge or for the 42-day detox. You could use it for both of them. If someone comes in and they say, no, I don't want your service, you say, hey, no worries, can I just give you six weeks of value for free? You're going to say yes. Of course you can. You can say awesome. I still want you to start with everyone. I still want you to be a part of the community. The only difference is you're not going to work out here. You're going to work at home, but we're still going to give you the meal plans, the grocery list, food preparation instruction. We'll give you workouts to do at home. All this stuff, so value is added. Then you'll schedule a time for them to come in for the nutrition orientation just like you would if they had signed up. That's how it works. Then when they come in, you pitch them supplements just like you normally would with an assumed close. Ironically, the people who won't pay for services oftentimes will still shell out $200, $300, $400 plus for product. It's because the real reason they didn't buy is because they probably didn't want to do the work. They were afraid of taking that jump. Whereas with physical product, they feel like they can do it on their own. They're a little bit more space between them and you. You can still liquidate your acquisition costs that originally you would have just lost when they walked out the door. It allows you to make money from the people who are saying no for service, which allows you to add a whole new category of services that you're providing. These people on the back end will come and then at week four, you're going to try and meet with them to then hopefully sell them into a membership or continue to sell them on the continuity of staying on the subscriber and save for the product. If you look at the time spent versus how much you're getting for the sale, you're still making a 90% margin on a 15 or 20 minute meeting with them. It makes total sense financially for the business and now you can pay a rent off people who would otherwise say no. Let me make sure I cover all these. The reason there's one funnel but it's two different plays is because we already know the funnel works. You have one sure funnel that you will use for your internal clients that you will give them access to. Then if someone signs up from ColdTraffic, you're still going to give them the same product. It's just one way you're marketing internally, one way you're marketing externally. The product's the same. Why is the No Sale Sale different from the Six Week Challenge? The No Sale Sale is a process that is applied to any external client. If two months from now we come up with a six week booty challenge externally that converts, you could still use this. The No Sale Sale where they say, no, I don't want to sign up for your booty challenge. You say, hey, let me give you the booty challenge at home. You can sign up for it. We'll give you all the same stuff. You're just going to work out at home. The No Sale Sale is more of a concept of how you can apply it to ColdTraffic than it is like a standalone play. It is a process that you can now attach to the front of your business from your own forward to any person who says no to any service. You can then have them come in for a nutrition orientation and then sell them product. They said no to service but they didn't say no to the other things that you have to offer. You can still ultimately help them, which is great. If you provide value, they might come back and actually sign up. It gives you another chance to sign up for service. Can I advertise the 4280 Talks to ColdTraffic like the six week challenge? Yes. That's the beauty of this specific play is that we actually did test the ColdTraffic. It's working as well at this point, if not better than the six week challenge. Yes, you can. Not all internal plays can be marketed externally. This one can because it rocks. How do you fulfill the No Sale Sale? The No Sale Sale you fulfill, so when they come in for the nutrition orientation, just like you were with a normal client, everything is literally the same except you're not going to have them pick the times they're going to work out. Otherwise, you're going to make them a login. You're going to give them the login and password and the URL. When they come, don't give it to them on their first meeting. Give it to them at the orientation so they have something to come for. They're like, yeah, we're going to give you all the stuff on the day for your orientation. You're going to get your cookbooks, your meal plans, all the stuff so they'll come in. That's when you make them the login so that they can go create that. You're going to explain the meal plan, sell them the product. That's how you fulfill it. The accountability pieces that are there are actually inside of the portal. If you want to go above and beyond, I guarantee you'll make more money if you reach out to them like you do your normal customers. If you feel like that's too much, then definitely at least reach out to them at the halfway point to get them in so you can resell them a product before the subscription renews. What is the relationship between the detox and the no-sale sale? Are they solely used together? No. The no-sale sale is a separate process. It can be applied to any external play, the six-week challenge. If you do a 28-day challenge, if you do it like any external play, you can apply the no-sale sale concept to it. They say no to whatever the external play is. You say, hey, let me serve you for free. Just come on in for nutrition orientation. That way you still feel like you're part of the group. Let me give you some value. Let me at least help you out. No one's going to say no to that. It's free. If you want to come in, that's when you sell the product. Should I run external or internal first? It's a good question. My preference is to run internal first. Reason being, it'll give you a lot of practice. It'll allow you to create cash flow immediately for no ad spend. Then you can use the money that you make from your internal play to then cash flow, acquiring more customers. I am for doing internal first, sorry, internal first and then external because it's easier from a cash flow standpoint because you've already paid the cost of acquisition. It's literally just profit. It's just extracting more value. It's also good because then the vibe of the entire gym when new people are coming in is going to be talking about supplements. They're going to be posting the groups about it. The new customers are going to feel like they're a part of that. If I haven't started selling supplements, where do I go to do that? There should be a link underneath here that takes you to Supplements on Secret's training. All you have to do is follow the process that are laid out there so that you can absolutely crush it. That is the overarching view of the 42-day detox and the no sale sale, how they work together, how they're separate, but how you can maximize, how you can use the 42-day detox to make money from your existing customers, make money from your external customers, and use the no sale sale on top of that to just make even more money from the people who are coming in. Rock and roll.
